entries.title: WAICO Formally Launched at WAIC 2026: Xi Delivers First Keynote, 29 Nations Sign China-Led AI Governance Body
entries.summary: On July 16 twenty-nine countries—including Russia, Indonesia, Brazil, and Pakistan—signed the founding agreement for the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization (headquartered in Shanghai), and Xi's July 17 keynote called AI 'a symphony of international cooperation,' urged human oversight, and pledged 5,000 AI-training slots to developing nations, directly challenging the US-EU governance architecture.

links.title: Xi pitches China as AI partner to developing world — CNBC
Xi tells World AI Conference no single country should monopolise AI — The Next Web
China's Xi Jinping brings 29 countries together to reshape global AI — IBTimes SG

entries.title: China's Anthropomorphic AI (Companion) Rules Take Effect July 15: Doubao and Qwen Companion Features Shut Down for Hundreds of Millions
entries.summary: The world's first dedicated regulatory framework for AI emotional-interaction services became enforceable on July 15, immediately forcing ByteDance's Doubao and Alibaba's Qwen to discontinue personalized companion AI features—affecting hundreds of millions of users—because no platform's persistent-memory architecture could pass the framework's addiction-safety requirements.

links.title: China's regulation on AI companions takes force — IAPP
China AI companion law takes effect: Doubao and Qwen shut down — TechTimes
China's anthropomorphic AI rules tracker entry — AI Governance Institute

entries.title: China's AI Agent Framework Takes Effect July 15: First Dedicated Global Regulatory Category for Intelligent Agents
entries.summary: The Implementation Opinions on the Standardized Application and Innovative Development of Intelligent Agents—jointly issued by the CAC, NDRC, and MIIT—became enforceable on July 15, defining AI agents as a distinct regulatory class, establishing a three-tier decision-authorization framework, and requiring mandatory filing and compliance testing for agents deployed in healthcare, transportation, media, and public safety.

links.title: China AI Regulatory Developments: July 2026 Analysis — Rimon Law
China's agent rules and Illinois audits: Dual compliance deadlines — AI Governance Institute

entries.title: Thinking Machines Lab (Mira Murati) Releases Inkling: 975B Open-Weight MoE with Self-Fine-Tuning Capability
entries.summary: Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling on July 15—a 975B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model (41B active), Apache 2.0 licensed, trained on 45 trillion tokens with a 1M-token context window—and demonstrated a self-fine-tuning capability in which the model autonomously wrote and executed its own fine-tuning job, introducing governance questions about agentic model modification and change provenance.

links.title: Inkling: Our open-weights model — Thinking Machines Lab
Thinking Machines amps up its bet with its first open model, Inkling — TechCrunch
Inkling: Murati's first open-weight model — The Next Web

entries.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro Misses Third Consecutive Launch Deadline as Google Pivots to Stopgap Releases
entries.summary: The rebuilt Gemini 3.5 Pro missed its July 17 target—its third consecutive slip since June—with reporting citing persistent hallucinations and inconsistent outputs that prevent it from clearing basic reliability standards, and prediction markets now place July 31 or later delivery at 73–81% probability.

links.title: Rebuilt Gemini 3.5 Pro misses third deadline: Google eyes stopgap release — TechTimes

entries.title: arXiv 2607.14353: Unsafe at Any AUC — Sociotechnical Disaster Analysis Applied to AI Risk Evaluation
entries.summary: Kroll et al. argue that lessons from Chernobyl, Bhopal, and the Challenger disaster—where risks were known but not acted on due to social, political, and economic pressures—apply directly to AI, and that responsible AI evaluation must transcend technical component metrics to analyze full sociotechnical systems.

links.title: Unsafe at any AUC: Unlearned Lessons from Sociotechnical Disasters for Responsible AI — arXiv

entries.title: arXiv 2607.14585: Cross-National Survey Finds Public Demand for AI Safety Systematically Misaligned with Dominant Regulatory Approaches
entries.summary: Lundgren and Tallberg's survey of public preferences across multiple countries finds that citizens—especially those who perceive AI as risky and personally consequential—prefer safety-first governance frameworks that existing regulatory approaches systematically fail to provide, revealing a structural gap between democratic preferences and policy design.

links.title: Governing Artificial Intelligence: Public Preferences and Regulatory Options — arXiv

entries.title: arXiv 2607.14037: Large-Scale Study of Early Adoption of Agentic Coding Tools Across GitHub Projects
entries.summary: Raida and Hou study how GitHub projects have adopted agentic coding tools, examining adoption patterns and the sociotechnical dynamics of integrating autonomous coding agents into open-source software development workflows—an area directly relevant to AI sociotechnical safety at scale.

links.title: Early Adoption of Agentic Coding Tools by GitHub Projects — arXiv

entries.title: Cloud Security Alliance Documents 10 AI Agent Security Incidents in 7 Weeks, Including Financial Trading Agent Data Poisoning
entries.summary: A CSA research report compiling 10 AI agent security incidents from January to March 2026—including a documented data-poisoning attack that caused an autonomous financial trading agent to recommend fabricated securities products to customers—concludes that enterprises lack the foundational controls for safe agentic deployment, specifically citing failures in identity binding, audit log integrity, and shadow traffic detection.

links.title: Ten real incidents in seven weeks expose critical gaps in AI agent governance — AI Governance Institute
Data poisoning attack forces financial AI agent to recommend fabricated securities — AI Governance Institute

entries.title: Tom Blomfield (Monzo Co-Founder, YC Partner) Joins Anthropic Compute Team, Citing Recursive Self-Improvement as Binding Constraint
entries.summary: Blomfield announced on July 13 that he is taking a leave of absence from Y Combinator to join Anthropic's compute team under Chief Compute Officer Tom Brown, saying compute availability is becoming the key constraint as the industry enters the early stages of recursive self-improvement—extending a sustained pattern of top-tier talent migrating to Anthropic ahead of its expected IPO.

links.title: Anthropic hiring spree 2026: Karpathy, Jumper, Blomfield — ExplainX
